mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate for equity valuations and tighten financial conditions, directly reducing S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show a positive mean during periods of rising FEDFUNDS, or Rank IC exceeds 0.05 in the opposite direction over a 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A sudden increase in the unemployment rate above 4.5% signals deteriorating labor market conditions and reduced consumer spending capacity, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 65% of instances where UNRATE crosses above 4.5%, or the signal shows zero predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates broad-based consumer demand strength, which drives corporate revenue growth and generates positive S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The joint positive signal yields negative mean forward returns over 60 days, or the interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample regression tests.

mechanism_narrative: Rising industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle reflects resilient supply-side capacity that outpaces rate hikes, supporting corporate margins and driving positive 20-day S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: INDPRO increases fail to predict positive returns during tightening periods, with a negative Rank IC over any 24-month rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP levels exceeding 18000 billion chained dollars confirm robust aggregate demand, which immediately boosts equity valuations and produces positive S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 55% of quarters where GDPC1 surpasses 18000, or the signal predictive accuracy drops below random chance in walk-forward validation.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stable or increasing interest rates forces aggressive monetary tightening, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: The combined high-inflation and rising-rate regime produces positive mean 60-day returns, or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained payroll employment growth above 200000 per month during a post-shock recovery phase signals strong economic momentum, driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth above 200000 correlates with negative 20-day returns during recovery phases, or the signal Rank IC falls below -0.02 in rolling window analysis.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in industrial production below -0.5% indicates a supply-side contraction that reduces corporate output expectations, triggering immediate negative S&P 500 returns within 5 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX yields positive 5-day returns in > 60% of months where INDPRO drops below -0.5%, or the threshold signal shows no predictive power (Rank IC < 0.01) in walk-forward tests.
